description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
On February 15, 2026 at 8:33 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (“Waymo AV”) operating in San Jose, California was in a collision 
involving a passenger car on Rio Robles at the parking lot entrance at 121 Rio Robles. 
 
The Waymo AV was traveling northwestbound on Rio Robles when a van that was traveling northwestbound in front of the Waymo AV 
with its right turn signal activated maneuvered right towards the curb and slowed at a parking lot entrance at 121 Rio Robles. The Waymo 
maneuvered slightly left to continue straight on Rio Robles. As the Waymo AV passed the van, a passenger car traveling westbound in the 
parking lot at 121 Rio Robles exited the parking lot without stopping at the stop sign and entered the Waymo AV’s lane of travel, and the 
front left side of the passenger car made contact with the rear right side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV’s 
Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage. 
 
Print
Clear Form
